label: Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations

node.label: Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations
node.definition: Executable and analyzable program structures support code generation, verification, and tool use.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations is to connect Programming languages, compilers & type systems, Algorithms & data structures to downstream capabilities including Neuro-symbolic & physics-informed learning, Boolean circuits & differentiable logic gates, Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.
examples: substrate for Boolean circuits & differentiable logic gates
substrate for Constraint propagation, logic & program execution
limitations: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Programming languages, compilers & type systems, Algorithms & data structures.

dependencies.rationale: Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Programming languages, compilers & type systems.
Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Algorithms & data structures.
